hooks.name: lint-on-save
hooks.description: Run linting checks immediately when files are saved, providing instant feedback to developers. Replaces 3-minute CI feedback loops with <1 second local validation. Supports ESLint, Prettier, flake8, pylint, golint, clippy, and other linters.

action.command:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LINT_COMMAND="${LINT_COMMAND:-npm run lint}"
AUTO_FIX="${AUTO_FIX:-false}"
FAIL_ON_WARNING="${FAIL_ON_WARNING:-false}"
CHECK_FORMATTING="${CHECK_FORMATTING:-true}"
CHANGED_FILES="${KIRO_CHANGED_FILES:-}"

echo "🔍 Running linting checks on saved files..."
echo "Files changed: ${CHANGED_FILES:-all files}"
echo ""

detect_and_recommend() {
  if [ -f "package.json" ]; then
    if ! grep -q '"lint"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "⚠️  JavaScript/TypeScript project detected but no 'lint' script found"
      echo ""
      echo "Recommended setup:"
      echo "  1. Install ESLint: npm install --save-dev eslint @typescript-eslint/parser @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin"
      echo "  2. Add to package.json scripts:"
      echo '     "lint": "eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx,.js,.jsx"'
      echo "  3. Create .eslintrc.json with your rules"
      echo ""
      return 1
    fi
  elif [ -f "setup.py" ] || [ -f "pyproject.toml" ]; then
    if ! command -v flake8 &> /dev/null && ! command -v pylint &> /dev/null; then
      echo "⚠️  Python project detected but no linter installed"
      echo ""
      echo "Recommended setup:"
      echo "  1. Install flake8: pip install flake8"
      echo "  2. Or install pylint: pip install pylint"
      echo "  3. Update LINT_COMMAND to: flake8 . or pylint src/"
      echo ""
      return 1
    fi
  elif [ -f "Cargo.toml" ]; then
    echo "ℹ️  Rust project detected - using cargo clippy"
    LINT_COMMAND="cargo clippy"
  fi
  return 0
}

if ! detect_and_recommend; then
  echo "❌ Linting not configured - skipping checks"
  exit 0
fi

FULL_LINT_CMD="$LINT_COMMAND"

if [ "$AUTO_FIX" = "true" ]; then
  if [[ "$LINT_COMMAND" == *"eslint"* ]]; then
    FULL_LINT_CMD="$LINT_COMMAND --fix"
  elif [[ "$LINT_COMMAND" == *"prettier"* ]]; then
    FULL_LINT_CMD="${LINT_COMMAND/--check/--write}"
  fi
  echo "Auto-fix enabled: will attempt to fix issues automatically"
fi

if [ -n "$CHANGED_FILES" ]; then
  if [[ "$LINT_COMMAND" == *"eslint"* ]] || [[ "$LINT_COMMAND" == *"prettier"* ]]; then
    FULL_LINT_CMD="$LINT_COMMAND $CHANGED_FILES"
  fi
fi

echo "Running: $FULL_LINT_CMD"
echo ""

LINT_EXIT_CODE=0
LINT_OUTPUT=$(eval "$FULL_LINT_CMD" 2>&1) || LINT_EXIT_CODE=$?

if [ $LINT_EXIT_CODE -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "✅ All linting checks passed!"
  echo ""
  echo "Code quality:"
  echo "  ✓ No style violations"
  echo "  ✓ No formatting issues"
  echo "  ✓ No suspicious patterns detected"
  
  if [ "$CHECK_FORMATTING" = "true" ] && [[ "$LINT_COMMAND" != *"prettier"* ]]; then
    if command -v prettier &> /dev/null || [ -f "node_modules/.bin/prettier" ]; then
      echo ""
      echo "Checking formatting..."
      if npm run format:check &> /dev/null || prettier --check . &> /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "  ✓ Formatting is consistent"
      else
        echo "  ⚠️  Formatting issues found (run: npm run format or prettier --write .)"
      fi
    fi
  fi
  
  exit 0
else
  echo "❌ Linting issues found:"
  echo ""
  echo "$LINT_OUTPUT"
  echo ""
  echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
  echo ""
  
  ERROR_COUNT=$(echo "$LINT_OUTPUT" | grep -c "✖.*error" || echo "0")
  WARNING_COUNT=$(echo "$LINT_OUTPUT" | grep -c "⚠.*warning" || echo "0")
  
  if [ "$ERROR_COUNT" -gt 0 ] || [ "$WARNING_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Summary: $ERROR_COUNT error(s), $WARNING_COUNT warning(s)"
  fi
  echo ""
  
  echo "How to fix:"
  if [ "$AUTO_FIX" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  1. Auto-fix common issues:"
    if [[ "$LINT_COMMAND" == *"eslint"* ]]; then
      echo "     npm run lint -- --fix"
    elif [[ "$LINT_COMMAND" == *"prettier"* ]]; then
      echo "     npm run format (or prettier --write .)"
    else
      echo "     Check your linter's auto-fix documentation"
    fi
    echo ""
  fi
  echo "  2. Review issues above and fix manually"
  echo "  3. Update linting rules if issues are incorrect (.eslintrc.json)"
  echo ""
  
  if [ "$FAIL_ON_WARNING" = "true" ] || [ "$ERROR_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Linting failed - code quality standards not met"
    exit $LINT_EXIT_CODE
  else
    echo "⚠️  Warnings present but not blocking (set FAIL_ON_WARNING=true to block)"
    exit 0
  fi
fi

metadata.purpose: Run linting checks immediately when files are saved, providing instant feedback to developers. Replaces slow CI-based linting with immediate local validation.
requirements: 10.2: Instant lint feedback loop (migrate from CI/CD to hooks)
metadata.addresses: Primary Concern 8: Fragmented Toolchains - Single unified hook with instant feedback

required: Update LINT_COMMAND to match your project's linting setup (ESLint, Prettier, flake8, pylint, golint, clippy, checkstyle)
Adjust FILE_PATTERNS matcher regex to match your codebase languages
optional: AUTO_FIX: false (default - report only) or true (auto-fix issues)
FAIL_ON_WARNING: false (default - errors only) or true (block on warnings too)
CHECK_FORMATTING: true (default) or false (skip format checks)
Adjust on_failure behavior: warn (show issues but don't block) or block_context (strict enforcement)

environment_variables.LINT_COMMAND: Command to run linting (default: 'npm run lint')
environment_variables.AUTO_FIX: Automatically fix issues if possible (default: false)
environment_variables.KIRO_CHANGED_FILES: Specific files that changed (automatically set by Kiro)

supported_linters.python: flake8: flake8 src/ tests/; pylint: pylint src/; black: black --check .; mypy: mypy src/
supported_linters.go: golint: golint ./...; gofmt: gofmt -l .; go vet: go vet ./...
supported_linters.rust: clippy: cargo clippy; rustfmt: cargo fmt -- --check
supported_linters.java: checkstyle: gradle checkstyleMain; spotless: gradle spotlessCheck

workflow_integration: CI/CD pipeline - Hook provides first line of defense, CI is safety net
Pre-commit hooks - Can coexist with git pre-commit hooks
IDE linting - Complements IDE-based linting with consistent rules

before_after_comparison.before_ci_based: Write code → Commit/push → Wait 3-5 minutes for CI → Discover lint issues → Context switch back → Fix → Commit/push again → Wait another 3-5 minutes. Total: 6-10 minutes per cycle
before_after_comparison.after_hook_based: Write code → Save file → instant lint feedback (<1 second) → Fix immediately → Save again → verified instantly → Commit with confidence. Total: <10 seconds

gradual_adoption_strategy.weeks_2_3: Fix existing issues, update .eslintrc if needed

performance.advantage: Much faster than CI (no queue time, no container startup, runs locally with no network latency)

best_practices: Start with on_failure: warn for gradual adoption
Configure AUTO_FIX: false initially, enable once rules are stable
Keep linting rules in .eslintrc.json (not hook config)
Use CHANGED_FILES to lint only what changed (faster)
Combine with formatting checks for complete code quality

troubleshooting.command_not_found: Install linter: npm install eslint or pip install flake8
troubleshooting.no_output_shown: Check LINT_COMMAND matches your package.json scripts
troubleshooting.too_many_warnings: Set FAIL_ON_WARNING to false, or fix issues incrementally
troubleshooting.performance_issues: Reduce FILE_PATTERNS to critical directories only
